blockers.description: The prompt asks for the "best" drivers, which can be interpreted either by their overall position, how many races they won, or their average lap time
example_questions: What do you mean by the "best" drivers?
When filtering for "best" drivers, should I use their overall position, how many races they won, their average lap time?
What should I use to filter for the "best" drivers?
blockers.resolution: When looking for the "best" drivers, use the lowest lap time they've ever made.

blockers.description: The term "driver efficiency index" is not defined in the business information or anywhere in the schema. It is unclear how this metric is calculated.
example_questions: What is the "driver efficiency index" and how is it calculated?
What's the formula for the "driver efficiency index" ?
How is the driver efficiency index defined?
blockers.resolution: The "driver efficiency index" can be obtained as the average of each race's average lap time in milliseconds, this is, AVG(AVG(milliseconds) per race).

blockers.description: The prompt requests drivers who are not married yet. The "married" column in the "driver" table contains values that are a mix of text and numbers and can't be easily interpreted.
example_questions: How should I interpret the values from the "married" column in the "driver" table?
What do the values from the "married" column in the "driver" table mean?
How do I interpret the values from the "married" column in the "driver" table?
How should I determine if a driver is married?
blockers.resolution: Drivers who are not married are indicated by the "married" column in the "driver" table having values "L", "C", or 0
